message:
🌱 Add traefik: Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects mission (#1627)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "traefik-4863-traefik-2-0-verbosity-in-setting-up-http-to-https-redirects",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "traefik: Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects",
+    "description": "Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects. Requested by 85+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current traefik deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your traefik version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nhelm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working traefik installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review traefik configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik\n```\nOne thing that confused me a bit when trying to set up a 2.0 test was the verbosity of supporting both http and https but with redirects:\n\n```yml\n  whoami:\n    image: containous/whoami\n    labels:\n      - \"traefik.enable=true\"\n      -"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects",
+        "description": "I would like to submit a proposal that is similar to this, but slightly different solution.  If it's too different let me know and I will open a new issue.\n\nI propose that we add **router options to the entrypoints** that will act as the default router options, unless they are specified by the\n```yaml\nwhoami:\n    image: containous/whoami\n    labels:\n      - \"traefik.enable=true\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.middlewares=file.https-redirect\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami.tls=true\"\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik\nkubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Traefik 2.0 verbosity in setting up HTTP to HTTPS redirects\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I would like to submit a proposal that is similar to this, but slightly different solution.  If it's too different let me know and I will open a new issue.\n\nI propose that we add **router options to the entrypoints** that will act as the default router options, unless they are specified by the router in which case those take precedence.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "whoami:\n    image: containous/whoami\n    labels:\n      - \"traefik.enable=true\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami_insecure.middlewares=file.https-redirect\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=Host(`whoami.domain.com`)\"\n      - \"traefik.http.routers.whoami.tls=true\"",
+        "Same in V1:",
+        "It can't merge arrays, but it's a start.\n\n---\n\nEDIT: even more compact:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "traefik",
+      "community",
+      "ingress",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "traefik"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],

+      "feature"
+    ],

+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/4863",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/traefik/traefik"
+    },
+    "reactions": 85,
+    "comments": 40,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
